mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: Unemployment rate increases by >0.5pp in 3 months but 20-day forward returns exceed 1.5% in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and rising inflation (CPI) signal a liquidity-driven market rally with overvalued equities, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns as volatility spikes.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS decline and CPI rise occur simultaneously but 20-day returns exceed 2% in 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth below 1.5% annually signals weakening economic demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines but 60-day forward returns exceed 5% in 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production above 105 index points indicates strong manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises above 105 but 5-day returns fall below 0.5% in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 295 signals persistent inflation, prompting central bank rate hikes that depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL exceeds 295 but 20-day returns exceed 1.5% in 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings improve.
failure_condition: PAYEMS rises above 150k but 20-day returns fall below 0.8% in 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 1.4 million units indicates strong demand for residential construction, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST exceeds 1.4M but 20-day returns fall below 1% in 60% of cases.
